name: FIBROMATOSIS COLLI
definition: PSEUDOTUMOR OF INFANCY = STERNOCLEIDOMASTOID TUMOR OF INFANCY = rare form of benign self-limiting benign fibrous mass within sternocleidomastoid muscle

demographics.Path: compartment syndrome with pressure necrosis + secondary fibrosis of
demographics.Histo: myoblasts + fibroblasts + myofibroblasts in various stages of differentiation
demographics.Age: 2nd to 4th week of life; M > F

dominant_finding: focal / diffuse homogeneous enlargement isoattenuating to other muscle
distribution: focal / diffuse enlargement of sternocleidomastoid muscle
discriminating_features: focal / diffuse enlargement of sternocleidomastoid muscle
NO extramuscular extension / associated soft tissue abnormality
mild mass effect on surrounding structures
lytic lesion in clavicular head at insertion of sternocleidomastoid muscle

general: focal / diffuse enlargement of sternocleidomastoid muscle
NO extramuscular extension / associated soft tissue abnormality
mild mass effect on surrounding structures
lytic lesion in clavicular head at insertion of sternocleidomastoid muscle
US: unilateral diffuse fusiform homogeneous expansion of sternocleidomastoid muscle
well- / ill-defined focal mass within muscle:
hypo- to iso- to hyperechoic depending on duration
mass moves synchronously with muscle at real-time
MR: isointense lesion to normal muscle on T1WI
hyperintense lesion relative to normal muscle on T2WI
subtle patchy and linear areas of decreased signal intensity
CT: focal / diffuse homogeneous enlargement isoattenuating to other muscle

clinical.Prognosis: gradual spontaneous regression over 2-3 months; spontaneous resolution during
